'''Jean-Marc Garcia''' is a French [[rugby league]] footballer who represented [[France national rugby league team|France]] in the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup|1995]] and [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cups]].

==Playing career==
Garcia played for [[AS Saint Estève]] in the [[French Rugby League Championship]].<ref>[http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=1142 1993 Test Match: Great Britain 72 France 6] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303232747/http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=1142 |date=3 March 2016 }} ''wigan.rlfans.com''</ref> He was first selected for [[France national rugby league team|France]] in 1991 and spent the rest of the decade as part of the side, playing in forty test matches.

In 1995 Garcia transferred to the [[Sheffield Eagles (1984)|Sheffield Eagles]]. At the end of the year he was named as part of France's [[1995 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]] side. Garcia remained with Sheffield for three seasons, leaving at the end of 1997 to rejoin [[AS Saint Estève]].<ref>[https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-super-powers-of-league-guide-to-the-top-divisions-12-teams-from-the-bulls-to-the-warriors-1153996.html Guide to the top division's 12 teams from the Bulls to the Warriors] ''The Independent'', 2 April 1998</ref> He again played for France in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cup]].<ref>[http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport/hi/english/static/in_depth/rugby_league/2000/world_cup/team_profiles/france.stm France] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20001216063800/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport/hi/english/static/in_depth/rugby_league/2000/world_cup/team_profiles/france.stm |date=16 December 2000 }} BBC Sport</ref>

French captain 1996–2000.
